title: pradosia cochlearia subsp. cochlearia
intro: Pradosia cochlearia subsp. cochlearia, also known as the "African Butter Tree", is a plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae, characterized by its unique, ear-shaped leaves and edible fruit. The plant is traditionally used in West African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Pradosia cochlearia subsp. cochlearia include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as providing antioxidant and cardiovascular support. Pradosia cochlearia subsp. cochlearia can be prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or as a powder by drying and grinding the plant material, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
